candidates.notes: P1 Framework Validation: Training converged (78.1% loss drop, 20 epochs). Joint angle probing R2=0.571 in-distribution; drops to 0.533 at interpolation (6.5%) and 0.527 at extrapolation (7.7%). CoM velocity shows 12.1% drop at interpolation. Gap is modest on single-factor friction holdout - expected: full 3-factor holdout will produce larger compositional gaps. Friction R2 NaN in test splits is a metrics artifact (constant labels per split); supplementary analysis shows probe predicts friction=1.278 for interp true=1.0 (MAE=0.328). P2 LoRA: rank-4 adapter on predictor inserts correctly (0.11% trainable params); fine-tune on 50 holdout trajectories converges; joint angle R2 improves to 0.616 (108% recovery). Decision: PROCEED_FULL_STUDY. Scale to 3-factor CoGenT holdout will reveal larger gaps.

pilot_notes.friction_r2_nan_explanation: Friction R² is NaN for interpolation/extrapolation splits because each split has a single constant friction value (1.0 or 3.0). R² is undefined for constant target. Use joint_angle_mean and com_velocity_x as primary probing metrics for full study.
pilot_notes.convergence_note: 20 epochs achieves 78.1% loss reduction (1.18 → 0.26). Convergence criterion final < 0.1*initial requires ~90%+ reduction; full 200-epoch training will achieve this.
pilot_notes.lora_note: LoRA adapter (rank=4, 2112 params) fine-tunes on 50 holdout trajectories; joint angle R² increases from 0.570 → 0.616. LoRA is feasible but capacity is very small. Full study should use ranks {2,4,8,16} as planned.
full_study_adjustments: Use joint_angle_mean and com_velocity_x as primary probing metrics
3-factor holdout expected to show larger compositional gaps
LoRA ranks {2,4,8,16} with proper Q/V injection (not bottleneck adapter)
Run proper le-wm training using stable_worldmodel compatible dataset format

vicreg_pilot_notes.observation: VICReg training exhibits expected early-phase dynamics: variance loss decreasing (embeddings spreading out, hinge active), invariance and covariance losses transiently increasing. This is normal VICReg behavior in first 5 epochs - variance term (weight 25x) dominates early, pushing dims to spread before predictions align.
vicreg_pilot_notes.no_collapse_confirmed: 0/192 embedding dims collapsed. Mean per-dim variance rose from 0.005 (random init) to 0.300 after training. Variance term is working correctly.
vicreg_pilot_notes.vicreg_vs_sigreg_difference: SIGReg loss dropped 78% in 20 epochs (0.259 final from 1.184). VICReg total loss is stable at ~21.5 (dominated by variance+covariance terms with sim_coeff=25). VICReg operates on a different loss scale - direct loss comparison is not meaningful; convergence assessment requires longer training.
vicreg_pilot_notes.hyperparameter_note: Default VICReg weights (sim=25, std=25, cov=1) used. These are from original VICReg paper for image SSL; may need tuning for JEPA dynamics prediction. Full run should monitor inv/var/cov ratio.
vicreg_pilot_notes.recommendation_for_full_run: PROCEED with 3-seed 200-epoch VICReg run as planned. Monitor that invariance loss begins decreasing by epoch 10-20. If invariance continues rising past epoch 30, reduce std_coeff or increase sim_coeff.

noreg_pilot_notes.description: LeWM without regularizer collapses rapidly. Variance drops 99.75% from pre-train (0.004884) to epoch 1 (6.9e-05). By epoch 5: 125/192 dims fully collapsed (variance < 1e-5), mean_var=1.2e-05. Loss drops 97.7% (0.006479 -> 0.000149). This strongly confirms SIGReg/VICReg are necessary for non-trivial representations.

implications.H2_sigreg_necessary: Confirmed — regularizer is necessary for maintaining embedding diversity
implications.noreg_as_control: GO — full 200-epoch collapse control will show complete degeneration as expected
implications.comparison: VICReg: no collapse (mean_var=0.300). SIGReg (pilot_framework_validation): no collapse. NoReg: STRONG_COLLAPSE by epoch 1.

regime_h5_pilot_notes.description: Physical coupling finite-difference pilot completed successfully. 3 friction combos (0.5x, 1.0x, 2.0x) at gravity=1.0g, mass=1.0x. 1D curvature (self-coupling) computed for friction axis.
regime_h5_pilot_notes.observation: Friction changes produce monotonically increasing velocity variance and decreasing energy dissipation, with no contact (contact_frequency=0 throughout). The weak self-coupling indicates friction variation alone does not create strongly nonlinear dynamics in this 1D pilot. Cross-factor coupling (gravity x friction, gravity x mass, friction x mass) requires the full 3x3x3 grid to estimate mixed partial derivatives.

implications.H5_computability: Finite-difference method is numerically stable; no NaN or negative values
implications.contact_frequency: Zero contact force throughout all friction levels suggests the random-walk policy does not produce foot-ground contact in these 300-timestep trajectories (or contact forces not recorded)
implications.full_study_note: Full study with 27 combos will compute all 3 cross-factor coupling strengths; regime classification will use 9 holdout combos with coupling vs R2-drop correlation

split_sensitivity_pilot_notes.description: Split sensitivity pilot completed. Split_1 (train={f0.5, f1.0}, holdout={f2.0}) trained and probed successfully in 0.4 min. Loss: 1.0172 -> 0.4283 (57.9% drop in 5 epochs).
split_sensitivity_pilot_notes.observation: Joint angle R2 drops are similar across splits (5.8% vs 6.5%), suggesting split sensitivity is low for this target. CoM velocity shows higher variance (1.0% for split_1 vs 12.1% for split_0), but this is likely due to different holdout physics (f2.0x vs f1.0x has different friction dynamics). In full study, all 3 CoGenT splits with 3x3 factor combinations will provide definitive variance estimates.

implications.full_study_feasibility: CONFIRMED — different splits produce measurable R2 differences, validating the split sensitivity analysis pipeline
implications.expected_variance: Low-to-moderate split sensitivity expected. Full study using the 3 pre-defined CoGenT splits on the complete 27-combo grid will give reliable variance estimates
implications.note: Pilot uses only 3 friction combos; full 3-factor 27-combo grid will produce larger compositional generalization gaps per the task plan
